item.title: ops/audit directory created without mode 0700 — agent decision traces world-readable on multi-user systems
item.intent: ops/audit directory must be created with mode 0700 at scaffold time so agent decision traces and tool-execution audit logs are not readable by other local users
item.description: scaffold.ts SCAFFOLD_DIRS array (line 38) includes 'ops/audit' — created in the standard loop at line 68 with default permissions (0755). Only 'security' and 'cloud' are in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET. ops/audit holds decisions.jsonl (agent decision traces from trace.ts), tool-execution audit logs, and egress audit logs. On a multi-user system, any local user can read the audit trail to determine what the agent did, when, and what tools it called. src/evolve/memory/trace.ts line 37 also calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without mode — but since scaffold pre-creates ops/audit, this is a no-op that doesn't worsen permissions. Root fix is in scaffold.ts SCAFFOLD_DIRS.
acceptanceCriteria: ops/audit directory is created with mode 0700 — no other local user can read agent decision traces or tool-execution audit logs
Existing files within ops/audit are not altered by this fix
All tests pass with no regressions
